name: UFAL Text and Speech Translation Group

overview.description: Machine translation and simultaneous speech translation research group at Charles University's UFAL institute
overview.description_long: The Text and Speech Translation Group (also known as the Machine Translation Research Group) is part of the Institute of Formal and Applied Linguistics (ÚFAL) at Charles University in Prague. The group researches neural machine translation, simultaneous speech translation, and multilingual NLP. Notable outputs include the CUBBITT system (Czech-English MT reaching human-level quality, published in Nature Communications 2020), the ELITR European Live Translator (H2020-funded simultaneous speech translation), and the Charles Translator for Ukraine (Ukrainian-Czech neural MT, 2019–).
overview.tagline: Breaking language barriers through machine and speech translation research

history.origin_story: ÚFAL (Institute of Formal and Applied Linguistics) was established in 1990 at Charles University's Faculty of Mathematics and Physics as a continuation of earlier computational linguistics research. The machine translation and speech translation group within ÚFAL has developed as one of its core research strands, gaining international prominence through EU-funded projects and top-venue publications.

key_milestones.event: ÚFAL founded at Charles University, Faculty of Mathematics and Physics
Chimera hybrid MT system developed, winning WMT 2013
CUBBITT Transformer system wins WMT 2018 English-Czech translation
ELITR (European Live Translator) H2020 grant awarded; Charles Translator for Ukraine launched
CUBBITT neural MT system reaches human-level quality; published in Nature Communications
Steven Krauwer award for Charles Translator team; regular IWSLT evaluation organiser
HPLT (High Performance Language Technologies) Horizon Europe project ongoing

leadership.name: Ondřej Bojar
leadership.role: Group Lead / PI (Machine Translation & Speech Translation)

open_roles.title: PhD / Master thesis supervision (Speech Translation, MT)

awards: Steven Krauwer Award 2022 for Charles Translator for Ukraine team
Nature Communications publication (2020) — MT reaching human-level quality

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Academic research group at a university; its mission is to advance fundamental MT and speech translation research, not provide a commercial product. The group actively works on frontier problems including simultaneous speech translation and multilingual NLP that are part of AGI research itself.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: As a grant-funded university research lab, market risk is not applicable. Funding comes from EU (H2020, Horizon Europe), national agencies (GAČR, GAUK, TAČR, MŠMT), and Charles University.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Funding through Charles University institutional support, EU grants (ELITR H2020, HPLT Horizon Europe), and multiple Czech national grants. As part of a well-established university institute (ÚFAL founded 1990), institutional funding risk is minimal.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: ÚFAL's Text and Speech Translation Group is a well-established academic research group with strong publication record (Nature Communications, ACL, IWSLT), sustained EU and national grant funding, and internationally recognized researchers. No commercial risk applies.

metadata.notes: No dedicated CNAIP page for this group. The 'Text and Speech Translation Group' name is used on the UFAL projects page (ufal.mff.cuni.cz/projects/machine-translation) to describe the Machine Translation Research Group. Group is part of ÚFAL (Institute of Formal and Applied Linguistics) at Charles University. UFAL was established in 1990; the MT group has been active since. Key grants: ELITR (H2020 #825460, 2019-2021), HPLT (Horizon Europe, 2022-2025), Babel Octopus (START, 2021-2023). Ondřej Bojar is the PI for translation-related grants.
